# Project title:  Improve Food

## Team/Product Name: Improve Food

## Day/Time/TimeZone for the scheduled team weekly meeting (30 minutes via Zoom)
Fridays 4:30 MST (unless otherwise specified)

## Vision statement: 
We connect communities with local restaurants through an app that makes rescuing great food affordable, convenient, and accessible to everyone. We add delivery options to a space dominated by pick-up only models to remove location barriers between surplus food and customers, reducing waste while putting quality meals within reach of more people in more places. 

## Motivation: 
The motivation for this project is to 1) reduce food waste and 2) improve food access with a business model that benefits all parties. 

Restaurants regularly discard food that is perfectly good but unsellable at full price near closing time or end of shelf life. That is lost revenue for restaurants and an environmental cost (wasted ingredients, labor, packaging, and methane impact). Providing an outlet for recoup revenue on surplus food is a direct financial incentive for restaurants to participate. 

Consumers are provided the opportunity to purchase high-quality food from local restaurants at a discounted price. Existing apps in the same (TooGoodToGo, Flashfood, Perishable Foods, etc.) are pick-up only. This model excludes people without access to transportation, mobility constraints, and those short on time. Adding delivery removes that friction and expands discounted food purchases to people who can actually benefit. 

## Risks to project completion, possibly including:
Creating a feasible business model by balancing reduced profits and delivery costs for restaurants. 
Project complexity is a potential risk as it will require a two-sided marketplace app (consumer + restaurant + delivery logistics). For this project, we need to sync inventory in real time, route items for delivery efficiently, process payment, and organize notifications. 

This team has no prior history working together. This could introduce differences in work styles, communication, and working hours. 

There is a risk of not finishing the project in the allotted time. Developing a two-faced marketplace app with a delivery component is complex and has many parts that may not reach full development. 

## Mitigation Strategy for above risks
Feasibility may be mitigated by creating an opt-in/out option for delivery services. Restaurants may also be given the option to set their delivery price. 

Project complexity may be mitigated by staging app development, using existing libraries/APIs, and delegating development tasks amongst team members.

No prior team history may be mitigated by scheduling regular meetings, updating team members on individual progress and roadblocks, and maintaining open, clear communication.

The time risk may be mitigated by setting internal milestones and visibly tracking progress. 

## Development method: scrum, kanban, waterfall: with specifics!
Scrum with Shared/Rotating Scrum Master: allowing the group to agree on a different Scrum Master for each meeting in order to keep everyone engaged and also to maintain flexibility with people’s schedules (some people may have time one week but not the next).

## Development Steps
Define user stories 
Develop consumer-facing interface 
Develop restaurant-facing interface
Develop backend software for loading user information, setting location, etc.
Develop backend software for loading restaurant information, food availability, etc.
Develop backend software for loading local restaurants within certain radius
Build pick-up/delivery timing algorithm
Integrate frontend and backend functions
Test and refine
